articles.title: Few Voters Say Trump’s Second Term Has Made the Country Better, Poll Finds
articles.abstract: A majority of voters said that Mr. Trump had focused on the wrong priorities and that they disapproved of his handling of top issues, but the president still enjoys strong support from Republicans.
articles.snippet: A majority of voters said that Mr. Trump had focused on the wrong priorities and that they disapproved of his handling of top issues, but the president still enjoys strong support from Republicans.
articles.byline: By Shane Goldmacher, Ruth Igielnik and Camille Baker

articles.title: America Cannot Withstand the Economic Shock That’s Coming
articles.abstract: America’s strength has always been its private sector. Empowering it can save us from A.I.’s worst effects on jobs.
articles.snippet: America’s strength has always been its private sector. Empowering it can save us from A.I.’s worst effects on jobs.

articles.title: Remixing Trump’s Economy Speech, Vance Strikes a Different Tone
articles.abstract: Vice President JD Vance pleaded with everybody to just hang in there a little longer, saying the economy would improve.
articles.snippet: Vice President JD Vance pleaded with everybody to just hang in there a little longer, saying the economy would improve.

articles.title: I Am Ambivalent About My Boyfriend. Who Can I Talk to About It?
articles.abstract: A reader wonders what to make of the reservations she’s having about a yearlong relationship, and whom to share them with.
articles.snippet: A reader wonders what to make of the reservations she’s having about a yearlong relationship, and whom to share them with.

articles.title: Losing Your Job Can Be a Mental Health Crisis. Here’s How to Cope.
articles.abstract: The emotional impact of unemployment can be profound, experts say — but speaking up about it and building a support system can help.
articles.snippet: The emotional impact of unemployment can be profound, experts say — but speaking up about it and building a support system can help.

articles.title: Read All About It: True Tales of Men and Fiction
articles.abstract: Readers respond to a column by Maureen Dowd about men and books. Also: President Trump’s tariff games; relationships outside romance.
articles.snippet: Readers respond to a column by Maureen Dowd about men and books. Also: President Trump’s tariff games; relationships outside romance.

articles.title: Bernie Sanders: ‘There Ain’t Much of a Democratic Party’
articles.abstract: The Vermont senator on how to take the country back from elites — on both sides of the aisle.
articles.snippet: The Vermont senator on how to take the country back from elites — on both sides of the aisle.
articles.byline: By Bernie Sanders, David Leonhardt and Jillian Weinberger

articles.title: ‘Show Up for Us’: 13 Democratic Voters on Trump, ICE and Their Frustrations With the Democratic Party
articles.abstract: The group discusses the Democratic Party’s response to Trump and his reshaping of American life.
articles.snippet: The group discusses the Democratic Party’s response to Trump and his reshaping of American life.
articles.byline: By Katherine Miller, Margie Omero and Adrian J. Rivera

articles.title: There Is No Trump Without the South
articles.abstract: Three Southern Opinion columnists on the region and its outsize role in national politics.
articles.snippet: Three Southern Opinion columnists on the region and its outsize role in national politics.
articles.byline: By Jamelle Bouie, David French, Tressie McMillan Cottom and Derek Arthur

articles.title: How Fast Will A.I. Agents Rip Through the Economy?
articles.abstract: The Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark tells Ezra Klein what he sees coming in the new era of A.I. agents.
articles.snippet: The Anthropic co-founder Jack Clark tells Ezra Klein what he sees coming in the new era of A.I. agents.
articles.byline: By Ezra Klein and Rollin Hu
